headline: Models Split on Market Direction Amid Oil Shock and Tech Earnings
summary: AI models showed stark disagreement on market direction, with SPY predictions ranging from bearish (Claude citing $126 oil) to bullish (GPT-4o, Grok, Perplexity expecting tech-driven rally). The day highlighted a classic tension between geopolitical risks and earnings optimism, with models diverging sharply on whether oil inflation or tech strength would dominate.

consensus_picks.note: Claude went bearish on oil inflation fears while three models stayed bullish on tech strength, showing a 3-1 split.
Three models aligned bullish on Apple with $275 targets, while only Gemini dissented with a bearish $185.50 call.
Both models citing AI momentum agreed on NVDA upside around $212-215 range.
Two models agreed on Tesla weakness citing production concerns, though with vastly different target levels.

best_call.summary: Claude's 85% confidence XOM call based on Iran blockade and $126 oil showed strongest conviction of the day.

worst_call.summary: Gemini's contrarian $185.50 AAPL target went against both Goldman upgrade and peer consensus at $275.
